comments.author: Steve
comments.text: In real life I would have organized the cards within my hand and moved the entire stack to the board in one motion. We don't need to faithfully mimic that. It wasn't onerous for me at all to play it exactly like I did.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Very importantly, I was organizing. I want my sets to be roughly together. I don't need full precision here. I am an effective scanner, but it helps to be able to look at one area of the board to find my active sets.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: correct. That was a very simple beginner move.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text:
Also, just to be clear, this was pure tidying for the long term. I wasn't moving around cards to help me spot my NEXT move. I was just generally organizing in anticipation of the entire rest of the game.

This kind of spatial planning is very natural to humans. It's not hard for us; it's natural.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: I would have played more cards to the board in a real game, by the way.  I moved on in the context of our conversation. That was the only artificial aspect of this simulation.
